forecast-period.text: A low pressure system south of the Bight will drift to the south east while a trough extends across western coasts. This trough will linger along the coastline into Friday, developing into the cold front that will clear into the east early Saturday. A high pressure system will begin to move in from the west on Saturday, cross the Bight, slowly drifting to eastern SA by Monday.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for South Central Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning southerly in the late morning and afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 to 2.5 metres, increasing to 2.5 to 4 metres during the morning, then tending southerly 4 metres later in the evening.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 25 knots decreasing to 10 to 15 knots in the middle of the day.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
Southerly 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres around midday, then tending south to southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres during the afternoon.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 2 metres around midday.
Partly cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to below 10 knots during the day.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the afternoon or evening.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.

area.description: Upper South East Coast: Murray Mouth to Cape Jaffa

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Upper South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Westerly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots during the morning and early afternoon. Winds turning south to southwesterly 15 to 25 knots during the morning.
1 to 2 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 3 metres during the morning, then increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the afternoon.
Cloudy. Near 100% chance of showers.
Southerly 15 to 20 knots, reaching up to 25 knots offshore early in the morning. Winds turning southeasterly 10 to 15 knots in the late afternoon.
1 to 2 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres around midday.
South to southwesterly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres by early evening, then tending southwesterly 2.5 metres later in the evening.
Below 0.5 metres, tending southerly 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 90% chance of showers.
South to southeasterly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning.
Below 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 2.5 metres.
Southerly 1 to 1.5 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the morning.
Partly cloudy. 60% chance of showers.

area.description: Lower South East Coast: Cape Jaffa to SA-VIC Border

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots turning southerly in the early afternoon.
2 to 3 metres.
Southwesterly 1.5 metres, increasing to 2 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 95% chance of showers. The chance of a thunderstorm during the morning and afternoon.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Saturday for Lower South East Coast
forecast-period.text: Southerly 15 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots south of Beachport early in the morning.
1.5 to 2.5 metres, decreasing to 1 to 1.5 metres during the morning.
Southerly 3 to 4 metres, decreasing to 3 metres during the afternoon, then tending southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres by early evening.
Southeast to southwesterly below 1 metre, increasing to 1.5 to 2 metres north of Port MacDonnell by early evening.
Cloudy. 80% chance of showers.
Southerly 10 to 15 knots decreasing to about 10 knots during the morning.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2.5 to 3 metres.
Southerly 1 to 2 metres, decreasing below 0.5 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 50% chance of showers.
